scenario: Helix Fintech books 11000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8 with timestamps in Europe/Berlin but reports them in America/Los_Angeles local time. A FEEL expression in a script task must convert @"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin" into the equivalent Los Angeles local time of 05:27:30 before the value reaches the downstream reporting rule.
question: Which FEEL call converts @"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin" to America/Los_Angeles local time as 05:27:30?

options.text: Call date and time(date, "America/Los_Angeles") which keeps the wall clock at 14:27:30 and only relabels the zone of the returned value
Call timezone(date, "America/Los_Angeles") which adjusts the time to 05:27:30 to match the local time of the target zone
Call at timezone(date, "America/Los_Angeles") which adjusts the time to 05:27:30 to match the local time of the target zone
Call date and time(date, "America/Los_Angeles") which adjusts the time to 05:27:30 to match the local time of the target zone

a.text: Incorrect. The docs state that date and time(date, timezone) adjusts the time to match the local time of the target timezone when the input has a different zone; it does not preserve the original wall clock.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no built-in timezone(date, timezone) function in FEEL; the documented Camunda Extension for this conversion is date and time(date, timezone).

c.text: Incorrect. There is no at timezone(date, timezone) function in FEEL; the documented Camunda Extension for converting a value to a given timezone is date and time(date, timezone).

d.text: Correct. The Camunda Extension date and time(date, timezone) returns the value at the given timezone and adjusts the time to match the local time of that zone; the docs show @"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin" becomes 2020-07-31T05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles.

explanation: The Camunda Extension date and time(date, timezone) returns the given date and time value at the given timezone; when the input has a different timezone, it adjusts the wall-clock time to match the local time of the target zone.
